name: Sam Scott
headline: Undrafted Jets back buried with no NFL track record
body:
Scott profiles as a camp-body addition for the Jets with no draft capital on record and no advanced-stat footprint to date. He sits at the very bottom of a crowded backfield headlined by Breece Hall, with Braelon Allen, Isaiah Davis, and Kene Nwangwu all ahead of him for early-down, third-down, and return work. There is simply no defined role here, and the depth ahead is younger and more established, leaving practice-squad survival as the most realistic 2026 outcome.

With zero NFL carries, targets, or snaps to ground a projection, any fantasy case is pure speculation tied to a string of injuries opening a door he would still have to win. Even then, the Jets would likely lean on the proven options before a roster long shot. For all redraft and most dynasty formats, Scott is a clear waiver-wire name to ignore until he shows something tangible in preseason. Do not roster him.
